title: requests — HTTP middleware
description: Middleware this module contributes. Registered by `AccessRequestServiceProvider::boot()` via `Router::aliasMiddleware()`. Only `access.request_hint_on_denial` is access-request-specific; framework + tenancy + throttle middleware come from their own modules.

middleware.description: POST-response decorator. Runs AFTER the RBAC `permission` middleware (from access/rbac) refused with 403. Inspects the outgoing response body — if `code === 'access_denied'` AND the route's resolved model class is registered in `AccessRequestableRegistry`, appends the four `request_*` fields (`request_url`, `request_payload_template`, `resource`, `permissions_wanted`) and rewrites `code === 'access_denied.can_request'`. If any check fails, the response is left untouched (no `request_url`, no shape change). This is the Google-Docs 'you don't have access, would you like to request it?' hint.
sideEffects: None on the underlying resource state. The middleware is pure response decoration.
Increments `access_requests.request_hint_offered.total` metric labelled by (application_id, tenant_id, resource_type) when it decides to enrich.

middleware.description: Framework throttle applied to the submit endpoint. Rate: 30 submissions per (caller_id, day) on POST /access-requests; 60 decisions per (caller_id, hour) on approve/reject. Distinct from tenant-level throttles managed by workflow/approvals. A 429 response carries `X-RateLimit-Trigger: submissions_per_day` or `X-RateLimit-Trigger: decisions_per_hour`.

notes: `access.request_hint_on_denial` is applied GLOBALLY at the API middleware group level so every 403 emitted by any tenant-plane API route participates. It is CHEAP when the response is not 403 (fast-path returns immediately).

Attaching the middleware globally is safe because it never blocks a request — it only decorates 403 responses. If a route's response is 403 without `code === 'access_denied'` (a policy denial with a different code), the middleware leaves it alone.
The middleware never fires for platform-admin routes — platform staff use impersonation via access/delegation, not the request loop.

There is NO `access.request_hint_on_denial` for the `POST /access-requests` route itself — if the caller lacks permission to submit a request (e.g. their tenant membership was revoked), the 403 stays a plain 403; no meta-request-to-request-access.
